page: Microsoft Azure DC16es_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the DCEV6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage, but it is Premium Storage capable, allowing users to attach external high-performance persistent disks. There are no integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses bundled with this configuration.

Operating with dedicated CPU allocation, the DC16es_v6 provides predictable compute performance without resource contention from other tenants. The lack of local storage requires relying entirely on network-attached storage, which introduces a dependency on network storage bandwidth and latency. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the hardware profile represents a balanced resource density suitable for workloads requiring dedicated Intel processing power and moderate memory capacity without the premium cost of local NVMe drives.

The DC16es_v6 is suited for general-purpose workloads, enterprise applications, and database hosting where external Premium Storage is utilized. It is also appropriate for application servers, containerized microservices, and development environments that require consistent CPU performance and a balanced memory-to-core ratio.
description: Microsoft Azure DC16es_v6 is a dedicated x86_64 server instance from the DCEV6 family, powered by Intel processors and managed via Microsoft Hyper-V. It is configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable, enabling high-performance external disk attachments. With dedicated CPU allocation, it delivers predictable performance for general-purpose applications, enterprise workloads, and database hosting using network-attached storage.
